locus_id: TYRP1 R93C (chr9:12,694,273; no dbSNP rsID at publication)
rsid: not_reported (novel; absent from dbSNP and 1000 Genomes at publication)
pvalue: P=9.60x10-23 when R93C genotyped directly in GWA panel (lowest of all 589,242 SNPs, stronger than the top tag SNP); best recessive hair regression (genotype+age+sex) P=2.19x10-90, R2=0.464; conditioning on R93C abolished all locus signal
evidence_quote: we identify a cystine-to-arginine change at a highly conserved residue in tyrosinase-related protein 1 (TYRP1) as the single source of blond hair in Solomon Islanders
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; R93C, original functional characterization paper for this variant.
author_explanation_quote: arginine to cysteine mutation in exon 2 of TYRP1...highly conserved...predicted to be 'probably damaging' with 0.999 posterior probability by the PolyPhen2 algorithm

locus_id: rs13289810 (9p23 GWA tag SNP; representative of a 151-SNP cluster)
coord_build: 9p23 (GRCh37/hg19); exact coordinate not reported; within ~500 kb mapping interval flanked by recombination hotspots
pvalue: P=1.11x10-19 (strongest of the 151 SNPs at 9p23 in the case-control GWA)
population: Solomon Islanders (Melanesia, Oceania) — 43 blond vs 42 dark
evidence_quote: conditioning on R93C abolished the signal for association across this locus, confirming a primary role for the coding R93C change
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; tag SNP rs13289810, conditioning on R93C abolished the signal, confirming R93C (idx54) as the sole causal allele -- passenger, not an independent locus.
author_explanation_quote: To verify that 93C is the single causal allele driving the 9p23 GWA peak...conditioning on R93C abolished the signal for association across this locus, confirming a primary role for the coding R93C change
notes: Tag SNP explicitly explained AWAY as a passenger of the coding R93C signal via conditional analysis -- a mechanistic account of non-independence, not merely a nearest-gene label.
